key_insight: CSP-MACE-Å is a hybrid machine-learning potential that combines intramolecular, dispersion, and learned correction terms to approach DFT-level crystal structure prediction accuracy at much lower cost.
engineering_relevance: This enables much faster ranking of candidate crystal forms, making large-scale solid-form screening and free-energy-based derisking more practical in pharmaceutical and materials workflows.
method: Train MACE-POLAR on OMol25 for intramolecular energies.
Model intermolecular interactions with MACE-POLAR + XDM-like dispersion + a learned delta correction.
Use harmonic free energies to rerank polymorphs beyond static-energy ranking.
evidence_signal: The model was evaluated on 19 AstraZeneca CSP compounds and 28 blind-test compounds, performing comparably to PBE+dispersion and close to B86bPBE-XDM, while also tracking temperature-dependent polymorph stability for five compounds.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and coverage of the training/evaluation chemistry, so transfer to new crystal chemistries or unusual salts/cocrystals may require retraining or additional validation.
paper_title: DFT Accuracy on Crystal Structure Prediction with Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials
